Release Readiness Gates

Run these gates after a stack is installed and before telling an operator that the deployment is mining-ready. Container liveness is not enough: a node can be reachable while Postgres schema, peer state, sync state, or template generation is not usable for mining.

read-only source package tooling no service restart

Command

python3 scripts/release-readiness-check.py
./scripts/validate-rc-local.sh

The readiness script reads .env by default, queries the Compose postgres service for schema state, and calls the configured node RPC endpoint. The local RC validator copies tracked and unignored source files to a temporary directory and runs source gates there, so live ops/runtime state in an active checkout is not deleted. Both commands exit non-zero if any required gate fails.

Gates

GatePass ConditionFailure Meaning
Postgres schemaRequired pool tables, columns, and indexes exist, including credits_block_miner_unique on credits(block_hash, miner_address) and the block_submissions history table/indexes.The pool may start but fail accounting, maturity, payout, block persistence, or submit-outcome history paths. Missing credit idempotency can break duplicate-safe submit accounting; missing submission history breaks A/B evidence.
Node RPCgetNodeInfo responds with a structured object.RPC auth, endpoint, module configuration, or node process is not ready.
Mineable/syncedgetTemplateHealth reports mineable/submit-ready, or the older isCurrent fallback returns true.The node is reachable but not a safe mining backend yet.
Peer sanityAt least one active sane peer remains after filtering the node itself, empty or unparseable addresses, loopback, unspecified, and inactive peers.The node may be isolated or accidentally connected only to itself/local addresses, or peer discovery may be advertising unusable peers.
Template functiongetBlockTemplate returns required mining fields including height, parent, roots, coinbase address, and nbits.The pool cannot create valid Stratum jobs even if containers look healthy.
Mining RPC stabilityThe mineable/synced, peer sanity, and template checks keep passing across the configured short sample window.The node or RPC path may be flapping during startup, peer discovery, or IBD recovery.
Deployment portabilityThe local RC validator passes from a clean temporary source copy and the dashboard collectors use Python HTTP rather than host-only tools.The release may work on the maintainer machine but fail on Pi5, Linux AMD64, macOS Docker Desktop, or Windows Docker Desktop because local runtime state or missing host utilities leaked into validation.

Useful Options

OptionUse
--rpc-url http://host:38131Check a node endpoint outside the local Compose default.
--min-peers 3Require more healthy external peers for production acceptance.
--stability-samples 5Require more consecutive mining RPC samples before readiness passes. The default is 3.
--stability-interval 2Set seconds between stability samples. The default is 1.
--mining-address 0x...Pass the intended payout/mining address to getBlockTemplate.
--pg-url postgres://...Use a direct psql connection instead of docker compose exec postgres.
--skip-postgresRun only node/template gates, useful for offline RPC troubleshooting.
--jsonEmit a machine-readable result for CI, installers, or release scripts.

Release Use

# After docker compose up -d and after the node has had time to sync:
./scripts/init-pool-postgres.sh
./scripts/release-readiness-check.py --min-peers 1

# For CI or an installer:
./scripts/release-readiness-check.py --json

Do not bypass a failed gate by marking the deployment healthy. Fix the failing dependency or wait for sync/template readiness, then rerun the checker.
